accurate.why: Specific figures cited (e.g., $159M for investigations, $3.01B for construction, $370M for MR&T, $5.714B for O&M, $218M for regulatory, $200M for FUSRAP) all match the bill text.

no_hallucinations.why: The content described (titles, programs, specific dollar amounts visible in the bill) matches the bill text; some later-title figures cannot be fully verified from the excerpt but no contradictions found.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies this as an appropriations bill for energy and water development agencies for FY2025.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Notes the Natural Gas Act amendment granting FERC exclusive LNG authority, and amendments to water programs.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes reprogramming rules, fund restrictions, and general provision prohibitions.

effective_dates.why: Notes the FY2025 period (through September 30, 2025) and the funds remaining available until expended.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers Corps of Engineers, Interior, DOE, and independent agencies with key figures.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, descriptive language; the listing of policy riders (CRT, vaccine mandates) is factual.

concise.why: Quite detailed with extensive dollar figures; while appropriate for a complex appropriations bill, the level of detail may exceed what is needed for a high-level summary.

correct_entities.why: Army Corps of Engineers, Bureau of Reclamation, DOE, FERC, and Nuclear Regulatory Commission all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: All specific dollar amounts cited ($159M, $3.01B, $370M, $5.714B, $218M, $200M) match the visible bill text.
